Package calendar.model

Interface CalendarModel

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    SparseHashCalendar
    public interface CalendarModel
    Interface defining the contract for calendar data models. Provides methods for adding, querying, editing, and exporting events.

    Follows Dependency Inversion Principle - controllers depend on this abstraction rather than concrete implementations.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addEvent​(CalendarEvent event)
      Adds an event to the calendar.
      void copyEvent​(java.lang.String subject, java.time.LocalDateTime sourceStart, java.lang.String targetCalendarName, java.time.LocalDateTime targetStart)
      Copies a single event (or its entire series) to another calendar at a target start time.
      void copyEventsBetween​(java.time.LocalDate startDate, java.time.LocalDate endDate, java.lang.String targetCalendarName, java.time.LocalDate targetStartDate)
      Copies all events overlapping the provided interval (inclusive) to another calendar.
      void copyEventsOnDate​(java.time.LocalDate sourceDate, java.lang.String targetCalendarName, java.time.LocalDate targetDate)
      Copies all events on a specific date to another calendar.
      void createCalendar​(java.lang.String name, java.time.ZoneId zone)
      Creates a new calendar with the provided name and timezone.
      void editEntireSeries​(java.lang.String seriesId, java.lang.String newSubject, java.time.LocalDateTime newStart, java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd, java.lang.String newDescription, java.lang.String newLocation, EventStatus newStatus)
      Edits all events in a series.
      void editEvent​(java.lang.String originalSubject, java.time.LocalDateTime originalStart, java.lang.String newSubject, java.time.LocalDateTime newStart, java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd, java.lang.String newDescription, java.lang.String newLocation, EventStatus newStatus)
      Edits a single event occurrence.
      void editSeriesFromDate​(java.lang.String originalSubject, java.time.LocalDateTime startFrom, java.lang.String newSubject, java.time.LocalDateTime newStart, java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd, java.lang.String newDescription, java.lang.String newLocation, EventStatus newStatus)
      Edits all events in a series starting from a specific date (inclusive).
      java.lang.String exportToCsv()
      Exports all events to CSV format.
      java.lang.String exportToIcal()
      Exports all events to iCal format.
      java.lang.String getActiveCalendarName()
      Retrieves the name of the active calendar if one is selected.
      java.time.ZoneId getActiveCalendarZone()
      Obtains the timezone associated with the active calendar.
      java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getAllEvents()
      Gets all events in the calendar.
      java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getEventsInRange​(java.time.LocalDate startDate, java.time.LocalDate endDate)
      Gets all events within a date range (inclusive).
      java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getEventsOnDate​(java.time.LocalDate date)
      Gets all events on a specific date.
      boolean hasActiveCalendar()
      Checks if a calendar context is currently selected.
      int importFromCsv​(java.lang.String csvContent)
      Imports events from CSV format into the active calendar.
      int importFromIcal​(java.lang.String icalContent)
      Imports events from iCal format into the active calendar.
      boolean isBusyAt​(java.time.LocalDateTime dateTime)
      Checks if the user is busy at a specific date and time.
      java.util.List<java.lang.String> listCalendars()
      Lists the names of all calendars managed by this model in insertion order.
      void renameCalendar​(java.lang.String currentName, java.lang.String newName)
      Renames an existing calendar.
      void updateCalendarTimezone​(java.lang.String calendarName, java.time.ZoneId newZone)
      Updates the timezone associated with an existing calendar.
      void useCalendar​(java.lang.String calendarName)
      Selects the active calendar for subsequent operations.

    • Method Detail

      • createCalendar

        void createCalendar​(java.lang.String name,
                    java.time.ZoneId zone)
             throws DuplicateCalendarException
        Creates a new calendar with the provided name and timezone.
        Parameters:
        name - the unique calendar name
        zone - the timezone to associate with the calendar
        Throws:
        DuplicateCalendarException - if a calendar with the same name already exists
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if name or zone is invalid

      • renameCalendar

        void renameCalendar​(java.lang.String currentName,
                    java.lang.String newName)
             throws CalendarNotFoundException,
                    DuplicateCalendarException
        Renames an existing calendar.
        Parameters:
        currentName - the existing calendar name
        newName - the desired new name
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if currentName does not exist
        DuplicateCalendarException - if newName already exists
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are invalid

      • updateCalendarTimezone

        void updateCalendarTimezone​(java.lang.String calendarName,
                            java.time.ZoneId newZone)
                     throws CalendarNotFoundException,
                            DuplicateEventException
        Updates the timezone associated with an existing calendar.
        Parameters:
        calendarName - calendar to update
        newZone - new timezone
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if calendar does not exist
        DuplicateEventException - if conversion introduces overlapping duplicates

      • useCalendar

        void useCalendar​(java.lang.String calendarName)
          throws CalendarNotFoundException
        Selects the active calendar for subsequent operations.
        Parameters:
        calendarName - the calendar to activate
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if calendar does not exist

      • hasActiveCalendar

        boolean hasActiveCalendar()
        Checks if a calendar context is currently selected.
        Returns:
        true if an active calendar is currently selected.

      • getActiveCalendarName

        java.lang.String getActiveCalendarName()
        Retrieves the name of the active calendar if one is selected.
        Returns:
        the active calendar name, or null if none selected.

      • getActiveCalendarZone

        java.time.ZoneId getActiveCalendarZone()
        Obtains the timezone associated with the active calendar.
        Returns:
        the active calendar timezone
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if no calendar is active

      • listCalendars

        java.util.List<java.lang.String> listCalendars()
        Lists the names of all calendars managed by this model in insertion order.
        Returns:
        ordered list of all calendar names

      • addEvent

        void addEvent​(CalendarEvent event)
       throws DuplicateEventException
        Adds an event to the calendar.
        Parameters:
        event - the event to add
        Throws:
        DuplicateEventException - if an event with same (subject, start, end) exists
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if event is null or invalid

      • editEvent

        void editEvent​(java.lang.String originalSubject,
               java.time.LocalDateTime originalStart,
               java.lang.String newSubject,
               java.time.LocalDateTime newStart,
               java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd,
               java.lang.String newDescription,
               java.lang.String newLocation,
               EventStatus newStatus)
        throws EventNotFoundException,
               DuplicateEventException
        Edits a single event occurrence.
        Parameters:
        originalSubject - the original subject of the event
        originalStart - the original start time of the event
        newSubject - new subject (null to keep current)
        newStart - new start time (null to keep current)
        newEnd - new end time (null to keep current)
        newDescription - new description (null to keep current)
        newLocation - new location (null to keep current)
        newStatus - new status (null to keep current)
        Throws:
        EventNotFoundException - if event not found
        DuplicateEventException - if edit would create duplicate

      • editSeriesFromDate

        void editSeriesFromDate​(java.lang.String originalSubject,
                        java.time.LocalDateTime startFrom,
                        java.lang.String newSubject,
                        java.time.LocalDateTime newStart,
                        java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd,
                        java.lang.String newDescription,
                        java.lang.String newLocation,
                        EventStatus newStatus)
                 throws EventNotFoundException,
                        DuplicateEventException
        Edits all events in a series starting from a specific date (inclusive).
        Parameters:
        originalSubject - the subject of an event in the series
        startFrom - the date to start editing from (edits this and future)
        newSubject - new subject (null to keep current)
        newStart - new start time (null to keep current)
        newEnd - new end time (null to keep current)
        newDescription - new description (null to keep current)
        newLocation - new location (null to keep current)
        newStatus - new status (null to keep current)
        Throws:
        EventNotFoundException - if no events found in series
        DuplicateEventException - if edit would create duplicates

      • editEntireSeries

        void editEntireSeries​(java.lang.String seriesId,
                      java.lang.String newSubject,
                      java.time.LocalDateTime newStart,
                      java.time.LocalDateTime newEnd,
                      java.lang.String newDescription,
                      java.lang.String newLocation,
                      EventStatus newStatus)
               throws EventNotFoundException,
                      DuplicateEventException
        Edits all events in a series.
        Parameters:
        seriesId - the series identifier
        newSubject - new subject (null to keep current)
        newStart - new start time (null to keep current)
        newEnd - new end time (null to keep current)
        newDescription - new description (null to keep current)
        newLocation - new location (null to keep current)
        newStatus - new status (null to keep current)
        Throws:
        EventNotFoundException - if series not found
        DuplicateEventException - if edit would create duplicates

      • getEventsOnDate

        java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getEventsOnDate​(java.time.LocalDate date)
        Gets all events on a specific date.
        Parameters:
        date - the date to query
        Returns:
        list of events on that date (may be empty)

      • getEventsInRange

        java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getEventsInRange​(java.time.LocalDate startDate,
                                               java.time.LocalDate endDate)
        Gets all events within a date range (inclusive).
        Parameters:
        startDate - the start of the range
        endDate - the end of the range
        Returns:
        list of events in range (may be empty)

      • getAllEvents

        java.util.List<CalendarEvent> getAllEvents()
        Gets all events in the calendar.
        Returns:
        list of all events

      • isBusyAt

        boolean isBusyAt​(java.time.LocalDateTime dateTime)
        Checks if the user is busy at a specific date and time.
        Parameters:
        dateTime - the date-time to check
        Returns:
        true if any event overlaps this time, false if available

      • exportToCsv

        java.lang.String exportToCsv()
        Exports all events to CSV format.
        Returns:
        CSV string representation of all events

      • exportToIcal

        java.lang.String exportToIcal()
        Exports all events to iCal format.
        Returns:
        iCal string representation of all events

      • copyEvent

        void copyEvent​(java.lang.String subject,
               java.time.LocalDateTime sourceStart,
               java.lang.String targetCalendarName,
               java.time.LocalDateTime targetStart)
        throws CalendarNotFoundException,
               EventNotFoundException,
               DuplicateEventException
        Copies a single event (or its entire series) to another calendar at a target start time.
        Parameters:
        subject - event subject
        sourceStart - start time in source calendar timezone
        targetCalendarName - destination calendar name
        targetStart - new start time in target calendar timezone
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if source or target calendar missing
        EventNotFoundException - if source event missing
        DuplicateEventException - if copy introduces duplicates in target

      • copyEventsOnDate

        void copyEventsOnDate​(java.time.LocalDate sourceDate,
                      java.lang.String targetCalendarName,
                      java.time.LocalDate targetDate)
               throws CalendarNotFoundException,
                      DuplicateEventException
        Copies all events on a specific date to another calendar.
        Parameters:
        sourceDate - source date (in active calendar timezone)
        targetCalendarName - destination calendar
        targetDate - target date (target calendar timezone)
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if calendars missing
        DuplicateEventException - if copy introduces duplicates

      • copyEventsBetween

        void copyEventsBetween​(java.time.LocalDate startDate,
                       java.time.LocalDate endDate,
                       java.lang.String targetCalendarName,
                       java.time.LocalDate targetStartDate)
                throws CalendarNotFoundException,
                       DuplicateEventException
        Copies all events overlapping the provided interval (inclusive) to another calendar.
        Parameters:
        startDate - inclusive start date
        endDate - inclusive end date
        targetCalendarName - destination calendar
        targetStartDate - start date of the copied interval in target timezone
        Throws:
        CalendarNotFoundException - if calendars missing
        DuplicateEventException - if copy introduces duplicates

      • importFromCsv

        int importFromCsv​(java.lang.String csvContent)
           throws DuplicateEventException
        Imports events from CSV format into the active calendar.
        Parameters:
        csvContent - the CSV content to import
        Returns:
        number of events imported
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if no calendar is active
        DuplicateEventException - if imported events conflict with existing ones

      • importFromIcal

        int importFromIcal​(java.lang.String icalContent)
            throws DuplicateEventException
        Imports events from iCal format into the active calendar.
        Parameters:
        icalContent - the iCal content to import
        Returns:
        number of events imported
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalStateException - if no calendar is active
        DuplicateEventException - if imported events conflict with existing ones
